---
title: "Documentation"
---

## Complete Documentation

Welcome to the Candidate Matcher documentation. This guide covers everything from quick start to advanced features.

---

## Table of Contents

::: {layout-ncol=2}
### Getting Started
- [Quick Start](quickstart.qmd) - 5-minute setup
- [Installation](installation.qmd) - Detailed deployment

### User Guide  
- [Using the Matcher](user-guide.qmd) - Step-by-step tutorial
- [Matching Algorithm](matching-algorithm.qmd) - How scoring works
- [Notes Feature](notes-feature.qmd) - Managing candidate notes

### Technical
- [Database Schema](schema.qmd) - DuckDB table structure
- [Data Export](data-export.qmd) - Weekly refresh process
- [API Reference](api-reference.qmd) - For developers
:::

---

## Quick Reference

### URLs

| Page | URL |
|------|-----|
| **Live App** | https://ezraair555.github.io/candidate-matching/ |
| **Notes Viewer** | https://ezraair555.github.io/candidate-matching/notes.html |
| **GitHub Repo** | https://github.com/ezraair555/candidate-matching |

### Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `index.html` | Main matching application |
| `notes.html` | Notes viewer page |
| `app.js` | Matching logic + UI |
| `styles.css` | Corporate theme styling |
| `export_to_json.py` | Weekly ETL script |
| `schema.sql` | Database schema reference |

### Commands

```bash
# Test locally
python3 -m http.server 8000

# Export data manually
python3 export_to_json.py --duckdb-path /path/to/db.db --github-repo .

# Generate sample data
python3 sample_data_generator.py

# Setup repository
./setup_github_repo.sh
```

---

## FAQ

### General

**Q: Is this free?**  
A: Yes! GitHub Pages is free for public repos. Private repos with Pages require GitHub Pro ($4/month).

**Q: Do I need Python on my computer?**  
A: No! Users only need a web browser. Python is only needed for the weekly data export (can run on a server).

**Q: Can multiple people use this?**  
A: Yes! Share the GitHub Pages URL. Each user's notes are stored in their own browser.

### Data & Privacy

**Q: Where is my data stored?**  
A: Job/candidate data is in JSON files on GitHub. Notes are in your browser's localStorage.

**Q: Are notes private?**  
A: Yes, notes are stored locally in your browser and never sent to any server.

**Q: What happens if I clear my browser cache?**  
A: Notes will be lost. Export regularly as backup!

**Q: Can I sync notes across devices?**  
A: Not automatically. You'd need to manually copy localStorage data or use the CSV export/import.

### Technical

**Q: How often should I refresh data?**  
A: Weekly is recommended. Set up a cron job to automate it.

**Q: Can I customize the matching algorithm?**  
A: Yes! Edit the `calculateMatchScore()` function in `app.js`.

**Q: Can I change the colors/theme?**  
A: Yes! Modify CSS variables in `styles.css` under `:root`.

**Q: Does this work offline?**  
A: Partially. Once loaded, the app works offline, but you need internet for initial load and data refresh.

### Troubleshooting

**Q: Page shows "Error loading data"**  
A: Check that `jobs.json` and `candidates.json` exist in the repo root and were committed.

**Q: GitHub Pages shows 404**  
A: Wait 1-2 minutes after pushing. Check Settings → Pages is enabled with correct branch/folder.

**Q: Match scores seem wrong**  
A: Verify your data has required fields populated. Adjust weights in `app.js` if needed.

**Q: Notes not saving**  
A: Ensure browser allows localStorage (not incognito mode). Try clearing cache.

---

## Support

- **GitHub Issues**: https://github.com/ezraair555/candidate-matching/issues
- **Documentation**: Browse the sections above
- **Source Code**: https://github.com/ezraair555/candidate-matching

For questions or feature requests, open an issue on GitHub.
